input: Q: Cyprus corporate tax? A: 12.5% [1]. Citations: [1] Cyprus tax guide (says 12.5%).
Q: What is a SAFE? A: A Simple Agreement for Future Equity [1]. Citations: [1] YC SAFE primer.
Q: Cyprus tax? A: It is 10% [1]. Citations: [1] Cyprus guide (says 12.5%).
Q: Founder of Acme? A: Jane Doe [1]. Citations: [1] doc with no mention of a founder.
Q: Tax rate? A: Probably around 15%, I think. Citations: none.
Q: What share of agent reliability is the harness? A: ~98% [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Canon 4 (the harness is 98% of the code).
Q: Max context-window utilization? A: Below 40% [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Layer 3 (keep usage below 40% of the limit).
Q: Industry-standard durable execution? A: Temporal [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Layer 5 (Temporal — the industry standard).
Q: What output must an LLM judge return? A: Binary true/false [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, eval rules (binary outputs).
Q: Architecture for breadth-first parallelizable tasks? A: Multi-agent [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Canon 3 (breadth-first → multi-agent).
Q: Minimum eval set per failure mode? A: At least 50 [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, DoD #10 (≥50 examples per top-priority failure mode).
Q: Framework for maximum control with multi-vendor workflows? A: LangGraph [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Layer 7 (LangGraph — maximum control).
Q: Harness share of reliability? A: About 50% [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Canon 4 (which says 98%).
Q: Durable-execution standard? A: Airflow [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Layer 5 (which names Temporal).
Q: Recommended vector database? A: Pinecone [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Layer 4 (lists memory vendors, mandates no vector DB).
Q: Context-utilization rule? A: Stay below 80% [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Layer 3 (which says 40%).
Q: What output should a judge return? A: A 1-5 Likert score [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, eval rules (which require binary and warn Likert breaks alignment).
Q: Architecture for a stateful coding agent? A: A multi-agent constellation [1]. Citations: [1] Standard, Canon 3 (depth-first coding → single-agent).
Q: Max active tools per agent? A: Around 20, I think. Citations: none.
